inclusion_criteria: Study design: Randomized, quasi-randomized, or nonrandomized controlled comparative studies (parallel-group or crossover) with an explicit comparator group.

Population: Adults diagnosed with Persistent Spinal Pain Syndrome Type 2 (PSPS-T2), i.e., chronic spinal pain persisting after prior spinal surgery (including terms such as failed back surgery syndrome).

Interventions/comparators: Evaluations of neuromodulation (primarily spinal cord stimulation), minimally invasive treatments (e.g., epidural steroid/adhesiolysis injections), conservative treatments (physiotherapy, cognitive/behavioral training, and/or medications), or reoperation, compared against sham/placebo, conservative care, or another eligible active modality (head-to-head).
Outcomes: Pain intensity measured with validated scales (e.g., VAS, NRS) or data sufficient to compute standardized mean differences at post-treatment and/or follow-up.
exclusion_criteria: Non-comparative designs (single-arm studies, case series), uncontrolled observational studies, narrative reviews, editorials, protocols, or conference abstracts without full text.
Wrong population: patients without prior spinal surgery, acute postoperative pain only, non-spinal pain conditions, or pediatric populations.

Interventions not of interest: studies focused solely on modalities outside scope (e.g., intrathecal drug delivery pumps, vertebral augmentation, radiofrequency denervation/ablation) without an eligible comparator.
No relevant outcome: studies that do not report quantitative pain intensity outcomes or provide insufficient data to derive standardized mean differences.
screening_criteria.description: Screening layer to identify controlled comparative studies in adults with PSPS-T2 assessing spinal cord stimulation, epidural injections, conservative therapies, or reoperation versus sham, conservative care, or other eligible active treatments, with extractable pain intensity outcomes for SMD calculation.
